23. Asymptotics of Hypothesis Testing

As we have discussed, methods for approximate inference can be roughly categorized into three flavors: stochastic approximation, asymptotic approximation, and variational approximation (in practice, methods use combinations of all 3). In this section, we begin our development of asymptotic approximations, starting with (binary) hypothesis testing and leveraging the powerful analysis tools of typicality/method of types.

We use our ε-divergence typicality notion to analyze the likelihood ratio. Under , it should be at one spot. Under it should be at another spot. We use typicality to handle the and as a function of threshold and relate it to Bayesian and Neyman-Pearson.
